content: {"notes": [{"url": "https://pmc.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/articles/PMC10945168/", "title": "Intermittent fasting and health outcomes: an umbrella review ...", "summary": "Summary Background Benefits of Intermittent fasting (IF) on health-related outcomes have been found in a range of randomised controlled trials (RCTs). Our umbrella review aimed to systematically analyze and synthesize the available causal evidence on IF and its impact on specific health-related outcomes while evaluating its evidence quality. Methods We comprehensively searched the PubMed, Embase, Web of Science, and Cochrane databases (from inception up to 8 January 2024) to identify related systematic reviews and meta-analyses of RCTs investigating the association between IF and human health outcomes.", "perspectives": ["Scientific"], "key_facts": ["Methods We comprehensively searched the PubMed, Embase, Web of Science, and Cochrane databases (from inception up to 8 January 2024) to identify related systematic reviews and meta-analyses of RCTs investigating the association between IF and human h", "We recalculated the effect sizes for each meta-analysis as mean difference (MD) or standardized mean difference (SMD) with corresponding 95% confidence intervals (CIs).", "Subgroup analyses were performed for populations based on three specific status: diabetes, overweight or obesity, and metabolic syndrome.", "Twenty-one (91%) meta-analyses with 346 associations were rated as high confidence according to the AMSTAR criteria.", "The summary effects estimates were significant at p 50% as a criterion for downgrading by one or two levels for inconsistency,39,94 to assign the highest quality of evidence to robust associations without heterogeneity."], "knowledge_contribution": "Evidence: Methods We comprehensively searched the PubMed, Embase, Web of Science, and Cochrane databases (from inception up to 8 J"}, {"url": "https://www.kplctv.com/2026/04/02/benefits-risks-intermittent-fasting/", "title": "Benefits and risks of intermittent fasting", "summary": "Benefits and risks of intermittent fasting ORLANDO, Fla. (Ivanhoe Newswire) - According to a new study published in the Journal of the American Medical Association, the number of Americans living with obesity is expected to rise by 19 million and affect nearly 126 million people by 2035! Experts say intermittent fasting is a good way to lose weight. Journal If you\u2019re not reaching the number on the scale you want, you cut back on what you eat.", "perspectives": ["Scientific"], "key_facts": ["Benefits and risks of intermittent fasting ORLANDO, Fla. (Ivanhoe Newswire) - According to a new study published in the Journal of the American Medical Association, the number of Americans living with obesity is expected to rise by 19 million and aff", "More than 13% of Americans have tried it. \u201cYou eat only in a particular number of hours for the day,\u201d described Tara Collingwood, registered dietitian nutritionist at Diet Diva.", "Time-restricted eating, or TRE, is a form of this fasting.", "In fact, a study by the American Heart Association concluded that eight-hour time-restricted eating is linked to a 91% higher risk of cardiovascular death.", "Check with your doctor if you\u2019re diabetic or have specific health risks."], "knowledge_contribution": "Evidence: Benefits and risks of intermittent fasting ORLANDO, Fla. (Ivanhoe Newswire) - According to a new study published in the "}, {"url": "https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=X63_bZI7maQ", "title": "Does intermittent fasting increase heart disease risk?", "summary": "Mayo ClinicMayo Clinic is a nonprofit worldwide leader in medical care, research and education for people from all walks of life. Doctors from every medical specialty work together to care for patients, joined by common systems and a philosophy of \"the needs of the patient come first.\" Our headquarters are located in Rochester, MN, while we have campuses in Florida and Arizona. Looking to connect with others facing similar health issues?", "perspectives": ["Scientific"], "key_facts": ["Anyone shown without a mask was either recorded prior to COVID-19 or recorded in a non-patient care area where social distancing and other safety protocols were followed."], "knowledge_contribution": "Evidence: Anyone shown without a mask was either recorded prior to COVID-19 or recorded in a non-patient care area where social di"}, {"url": "https://www.nia.nih.gov/news/research-intermittent-fasting-shows-health-benefits", "title": "Research on intermittent fasting shows health benefits", "summary": "Evidence from decades of animal and human research points to wide-ranging health benefits of intermittent fasting, according to an NIA-conducted review of the research, published in the New England Journal of Medicine. Still, more research is needed to determine whether intermittent fasting yields benefits or is even feasible for humans when practiced over the long term, such as for years. Intermittent fasting is an eating pattern that includes hours or days of no or minimal food consumption without deprivation of essential nutrients.", "perspectives": ["Scientific"], "key_facts": ["Still, more research is needed to determine whether intermittent fasting yields benefits or is even feasible for humans when practiced over the long term, such as for years.", "Intermittent fasting is an eating pattern that includes hours or days of no or minimal food consumption without deprivation of essential nutrients.", "This activity enhances the body\u2019s defenses against oxidative and metabolic stress and initiates the removal or repair of damaged molecules.", "The authors suggest that clinicians who prescribe intermittent fasting encourage their patients to adopt a gradual, phased-in schedule in consultation with a dietitian or nutritionist.", "Studies are needed to determine whether this eating pattern is safe for people at a healthy weight, or who are younger or older, since most clinical research so far has been conducted on overweight and middle-aged adults."], "knowledge_contribution": "Evidence: Still, more research is needed to determine whether intermittent fasting yields benefits or is even feasible for humans "}, {"url": "https://hsph.harvard.edu/news/the-health-benefits-of-intermittent-fasting/", "title": "The health benefits of intermittent fasting", "summary": "The health benefits of intermittent fasting Intermittent fasting researcher Courtney Peterson joins Harvard Chan School and will direct Department of Nutrition\u2019s new research kitchen. In 2018, Courtney Peterson published what proved to be a landmark paper in the burgeoning field of intermittent fasting. The study, which found that pre-diabetic men could dramatically improve their blood sugar control by eating during a shorter daily window starting in the early morning, has now been cited more than 1,600 times according to Google Scholar.", "perspectives": ["Scientific"], "key_facts": ["In 2018, Courtney Peterson published what proved to be a landmark paper in the burgeoning field of intermittent fasting.", "A: We\u2019ve found that daily intermittent fasting\u2014eating regular meals during a specific window, for example, between 8 a.m. and 4 p.m. or 11 a.m. and 7 p.m., and fasting for at least 16 hours a day\u2014does help people lose weight, the equivalent of about ", "We don\u2019t recommend intermittent fasting during pregnancy or for children, and most studies on intermittent fasting exclude people with histories of disordered eating, just to be on the safe side.", "For instance, we often find that people in certain jobs, such as teachers or shift workers, have difficulty because they don\u2019t get good breaks during the day to eat.", "For people who want to do intermittent fasting, I recommend an eight-hour or shorter window, as this is where we really start to see benefits kick in."], "knowledge_contribution": "Evidence: In 2018, Courtney Peterson published what proved to be a landmark paper in the burgeoning field of intermittent fasting."}, {"url": "https://www.exonpublications.com/index.php/exon/article/view/intermittent-fasting-methods-health-benefits-risks", "title": "Intermittent Fasting Methods, Health Benefits, and Risks", "summary": "Intermittent Fasting Methods, Health Benefits, and Risks Main Article Content Intermittent fasting is an eating pattern that alternates between periods of eating and fasting. This comprehensive guide explores the principles behind intermittent fasting, various methods such as alternate day fasting, the 16/8 method, and one meal a day (OMAD), among others. It explores the health benefits associated with intermittent fasting, including weight loss and improved metabolic health, while also addressing potential risks and sustainability concerns.", "perspectives": ["Scientific"], "key_facts": ["DOI: https://doi.org/10.36255/intermittent-fasting-methods-health-benefits-risks Introduction Intermittent fasting has gained significant attention in recent years as a dietary strategy for weight management and overall health improvement.", "Eat-Stop-Eat (ESE) The Eat-Stop-Eat method entails fasting for a full 24 hours once or twice a week.", "The 16/8 Method The 16/8 method, also known as time-restricted feeding, involves fasting for 16 hours each day and consuming all daily calories within an 8-hour window.", "Water Fasting Water fasting involves consuming only water for a specific period, typically ranging from 24 to 72 hours.", "Juice Fasts Juice fasting entails consuming only fruit and vegetable juices for a certain period, usually 1 to 3 days."], "knowledge_contribution": "Evidence: DOI: https://doi.org/10.36255/intermittent-fasting-methods-health-benefits-risks Introduction Intermittent fasting has g"}]}
